title: Alcea transcaucasica
taxonomy: Alcea transcaucasica is a member of the plant family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow family. This family includes around 200 species of flowering plants, many of which are known for their showy flowers. Alcea transcaucasica is a type of perennial hollyhock that is native to the Caucasus region. It is often classified as a species within the genus Alcea, which includes about 25 species of hollyhock. Alcea transcaucasica is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within the Alcea genus.
uses: Alcea transcaucasica, a lesser-known plant species, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits.
